consequence: At any first crossing of F_alpha above 0, one would have F=0 and F'>0. Barrier majorization forbids F'>0. Hence F_alpha(t)<=0 for all t and the Talagrand shell-domination theorem follows.
name: Barrier Majorization Lemma
proof_route: Prove majorization only on the barrier/near-barrier region. Candidate reductions: KKT barrier, two-level threshold compression, or Veronese/Krawtchouk SOS for the shell gap.
statement: For w=12,16, if F_alpha(t)>=0 for the radial shell objective, then sorted tilted inclusion marginals satisfy cumulative majorization by the doubled coordinate Gibbs marginals: sum_{i<=k} r_i(t) <= w sum_{i<=k} p_i(t).
why_global_majorization_is_false: For w=16, prefix_16 at t≈6.5 gives positive derivative/majorization excess, but F≈-3.399, ratio≈0.0337, far below the barrier.
